symbols.name: N
symbols.def: number of units in the finite projection panel

symbols.name: I_N
symbols.def: unit set of the finite projection panel

symbols.name: g
symbols.def: first-treatment cohort, with infinity denoting never treated

symbols.name: D_it
symbols.def: treatment status of unit i at time t

symbols.name: Y_it(d)
symbols.def: time-t outcome under treatment state d

symbols.name: P_N
symbols.def: finite-panel law for the population projection

symbols.name: gamma_t
symbols.def: time baseline log mean with gamma_1=0

symbols.name: F_it
symbols.def: all N unit indicators and time indicators for periods 2,...,T

symbols.name: delta^(h;g,t)
symbols.def: delta with coordinate delta_gt replaced by delta_gt+h

symbols.name: E
symbols.def: early-treatment label in the two-cohort construction

symbols.name: L
symbols.def: late-treatment label in the two-cohort construction

symbols.name: epsilon
symbols.def: common positive log effect in the local witness

assumptions.condition: nu_it=b_it exp(delta_gt) for every (g,t) in C_N and every i with G_i=g.
ell_N has an interior normalized minimizer theta_N^*.
H_N is positive definite on the normalized coefficient space.

definitions.name: Naive unit-and-time-FE PPML projection
definitions.construction: theta_N^*=argmin_theta ell_N(theta), beta_N^*=e_beta'theta_N^*, and ell_N(theta)=E_N[mu_it(theta)-Y_it log mu_it(theta)]

definitions.name: Finite-panel counterfactual-share PTT
definitions.construction: PTT_N=E_N[D_it nu_it]/E_N[D_it b_it]-1=sum_{(g,t) in C_N}{E_N[1{G_i=g}1{s=t}b_is]/E_N[D_it b_it]}{exp(delta_gt)-1}

definitions.name: Positive-effect local forbidden-comparison witness
definitions.construction: Set N=2, T=3, I_2={E,L}, G_E=2, G_L=3, and b_Es=b_Ls=1 for s=1,2,3. Set delta_22=delta_23=delta_33=epsilon with epsilon>0, let r=exp(epsilon), and perturb only delta_23 to epsilon+h for h in an open interval containing 0 with epsilon+h>0.

definitions.name: Global PPML sign-frontier handle
definitions.construction: For a fixed timing array (G_i)_{i in I_N} and baseline array (b_it)_{i in I_N,t=1,...,T}, start at the homogeneous ray delta_gt=d, continue the score-defined theta_N^*(delta), and partition the strictly positive effect orthant by the sign of beta_N^*(delta); eliminate theta_N^* from the score equations to express nonemptiness of the negative region using only timing-cell counts and baseline masses.

statements.statement: Under ass:finite-irreversible-adoption, ass:finite-multiplicative-baseline, ass:finite-proportional-effects, ass:finite-projection-existence, and ass:finite-information-rank, theta_N^* is the unique normalized solution of E_N[Z_it{nu_it-mu_it(theta)}]=0 and, for every (g,t) in C_N, d beta_N^*(delta^(h;g,t))/dh evaluated at h=0 equals E_N[1{G_i=g}1{s=t}nu_is Wtilde_is]/E_N[mu_it(theta_N^*)Wtilde_it^2]. Its sign is sign(Wbar_gt). In def:two-by-three-local-witness, d beta_N^*/d delta_23=-r/(r+1)<-1/2 at h=0, so an open interval of strictly positive delta_23 values exists on which increasing the early cohort's period-3 proportional effect strictly lowers beta_N^*. If delta_gt=d for all (g,t) in C_N, then beta_N^*=d.
statements.justification: This supporting proposition supplies the local geometry and the homogeneous-effect reduction required to formulate a global PPML sign frontier.
statements.gap: MoreauKastler2025PTT, equations (3.4)-(3.6) and Appendix B1, analytically derives the two-group PPML likelihood system. This proposition records its finite-panel weighted-FWL differentiation in common notation but does not claim a strict extension of that local analysis.
statements.consumer: The frontier question uses the proposition to distinguish a local forbidden direction from a globally negative pooled coefficient.

statements.statement: For which finite staggered timing arrays and strictly positive multiplicative baseline arrays is the set {delta in R^|C_N|: delta_gt>0 for every (g,t) in C_N and beta_N^*(delta)<0} nonempty? Derive a necessary-and-sufficient condition using only timing-cell counts and baseline masses, and determine whether a negative Wbar_gt along a score path from homogeneous effects is sufficient for nonemptiness.
statements.justification: A negative local derivative can coexist with a positive pooled coefficient, so the primitive boundary of the global all-positive-effect pathology is the unresolved object.
statements.gap: MoreauKastler2025PTT section 3.1 and equation (3.6) establish the two-cohort PPML symptom; they do not characterize the primitive timing-and-baseline region in which a negative pooled PPML coefficient is globally attainable. Linear signed-weight conditions in deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE cannot supply this frontier because PPML residualization changes with the pseudo-true mean.
statements.consumer: An empirical staggered-adoption design can be screened for structural exposure to a sign-reversing pooled PPML coefficient before it is used as a causal summary.

sampling_model.design: The finite law P_N defines a population KL projection; no sampling limit is asserted for beta_N^*.
sampling_model.units: Units i in I_N are observed for all t=1,...,T.
sampling_model.treatment: Treatment is irreversible and begins at G_i.
sampling_model.overlap: Every cell in C_N has positive finite-panel cohort mass.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: There is no estimator in the frontier object; theta_N^* is the population score root.

tldr: The headline object is a primitive sign frontier for the naive unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient under staggered adoption and positive heterogeneous proportional effects. The supporting local calculation identifies the effect-dependent weighted-FWL direction: in the two-cohort/three-period witness, increasing one positive effect changes the pooled coefficient at rate -r/(r+1), strictly below minus one half. The causal counterpoint is the positive counterfactual-share PTT_N, but the proposal asks when the noncausal PPML projection itself can cross below zero despite every proportional effect being positive.

project_justification.gap: Published PPML work establishes a two-cohort wrong-comparison symptom but not the timing-and-baseline frontier for an all-positive-effect sign reversal.
project_justification.niche: Unlike linear TWFE, the PPML projection weights are endogenous to the pseudo-true mean, so a signed linear-weight theorem cannot classify global sign reversal.
project_justification.fill: The core fixes the population score geometry and asks for the primitive nonemptiness criterion of the negative-coefficient region.

related_work: MoreauKastler2025PTT is the direct anchor. Its equations (3.4)-(3.6) and Appendix B1 derive the analytic two-by-three PPML system, and section 3.1 identifies the wrong-comparison symptom; it does not give a timing-and-baseline characterization of the global negative region. NagengastYotov2025 is the closest fully interacted PPML implementation: its extended-TWFE gravity design estimates heterogeneous staggered trade-agreement effects, but it does not study the misspecified pooled PPML sign frontier or the counterfactual-share ratio E_N[D_it nu_it]/E_N[D_it b_it]-1. White1982MisspecifiedML supplies the pseudo-true-score framework. Wooldridge2023NonlinearDiD supplies the multiplicative parallel-trends mean structure. deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE and GoodmanBacon2021DiD are linear benchmarks whose fixed signed weights and 2x2 decompositions do not characterize an effect-dependent PPML score root. BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024EventStudy informs the distinction between causal imputation targets and contaminated pooled staggered coefficients.
honest_scope: The local weighted-FWL derivative is supporting geometry, not the contribution claim. The open object is the global primitive iff frontier. Under homogeneous proportional effects, the projection reduces to beta_N^*=d. No claim is made here about analytical inference for PTT_N or about a general PPML weighted-average decomposition.

bibliography.citation: Moreau-Kastler, N. (2025). Proportional Treatment Effects in Staggered Settings: An Approach for Poisson Pseudo-Maximum Likelihood. EU Tax Observatory Working Paper 31. doi:10.2139/ssrn.5247936.

Nagengast, A. J. and Y. V. Yotov (2025). Staggered Difference-in-Differences in Gravity Settings: Revisiting the Effects of Trade Agreements. American Economic Journal: Applied Economics 17(1), 271-296. doi:10.1257/app.20230089.
Wooldridge, J. M. (2023). Simple Approaches to Nonlinear Difference-in-Differences with Panel Data. The Econometrics Journal 26(3), C31-C66. doi:10.1093/ectj/utad016.
White, H. (1982). Maximum Likelihood Estimation of Misspecified Models. Econometrica 50(1), 1-25. doi:10.2307/1912526.
de Chaisemartin, C. and X. D'Haultfoeuille (2020). Two-Way Fixed Effects Estimators with Heterogeneous Treatment Effects. American Economic Review 110(9), 2964-2996. doi:10.1257/aer.20181169.
Goodman-Bacon, A. (2021). Difference-in-Differences with Variation in Treatment Timing. Journal of Econometrics 225(2), 254-277. doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2021.03.014.
Borusyak, K., X. Jaravel, and J. Spiess (2024). Revisiting Event-Study Designs: Robust and Efficient Estimation. Review of Economic Studies 91(6), 3253-3285. doi:10.1093/restud/rdae007.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Equations (3.4)-(3.6) and Appendix B1 derive a two-cohort PPML wrong-comparison system; section 3.1 diagnoses the staggered symptom and the paper defines a counterfactual-share ratio.
An extended-TWFE PPML gravity estimator implements heterogeneous staggered-DiD effects for trade agreements.
Regular misspecified QMLE has a pseudo-true parameter characterized by population scores.
Canonical-link nonlinear DiD identifies cohort-time effects under multiplicative parallel trends.
Linear TWFE has signed group-time-effect weights and can be negative when all effects are positive.
Linear staggered-adoption TWFE is a weighted average of 2x2 DiD comparisons.
Linear imputation event-study methods separate causal heterogeneous-effect targets from contaminated conventional regressions.

literature_checklist.author: Moreau-Kastler
literature_checklist.one_line: Equations (3.4)-(3.6) and Appendix B1 provide the analytic 2x3 PPML comparator; section 3.1 does not give a global sign frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Nagengast and Yotov
literature_checklist.one_line: Extended-TWFE PPML gravity implementation is the closest fully interacted staggered-PPML comparator.

literature_checklist.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille
literature_checklist.one_line: Linear negative-weight benchmark that does not yield the nonlinear frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess
literature_checklist.one_line: Linear imputation comparator for separating causal targets from conventional pooled regressions.
